channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Brazil Faces Stablecoin Tax Fight, Hub Status at Risk
item.description: Brazil's crypto industry is mobilizing against a proposed financial transaction tax (IOF) on stablecoins, arguing it violates constitutional law and the 2022 Virtual Assets Law by misclassifying digital assets as foreign currency. This move threatens to undermine Brazil's position as a regulated crypto hub, despite significant market growth and adoption. Industry groups warn of legal disputes and a chilling effect on innovation.

item.title: India JV Aims for Global Isotope Supply: CIL & Chemtatva Invest ₹300 Cr
item.description: Cambridge Isotope Laboratories (CIL) and Chemtatva Chiral Solutions are forming a ₹300 crore joint venture in Telangana's Genome Valley to establish advanced manufacturing for stable isotope-labeled compounds. The initiative aims to create over 250 jobs and significantly reduce India's reliance on imports for these critical materials used in pharmaceuticals, diagnostics, and advanced electronics. This strategic move positions India to bolster its domestic capabilities and potentially challenge established global players in a high-value, specialized chemical segment.

item.title: Elpro International: GST Search Concludes With No Material Impact Reported
item.description: Elpro International Ltd informed exchanges that Central GST authorities conducted searches at its locations on March 13, 2026. The company stated the proceedings concluded the same day, with Elpro providing full cooperation. No material impact on financials or operations is currently reported.

item.title: Krishival Foods Promoter Buys ₹12.80 Lakh Shares, Ups Stake to 34.49%
item.description: Aparna Sujit Bangar, Promoter and Managing Director of Krishival Foods, has acquired 4,000 shares worth ₹12.80 lakh. This transaction, representing 0.02% of the total paid-up share capital, nudges her direct holding to 34.49%. The move signals management confidence amid the company's expansion plans.

item.title: Tamil Nadu's TIDCO Rebrands to Venture Catalyst, Funds Deep Tech with ₹50 Cr
item.description: Tamil Nadu Industrial Development Corporation (TIDCO) has initiated its Startup Investment Policy 2025 with ₹50 crore invested in space-tech firm Agnikul Cosmos and electric mobility startup Raptee Energy, ₹25 crore each. This marks TIDCO's rebranding to 'Venture Catalyst,' signaling a strategic pivot towards directly supporting deep-tech and sunrise sectors. These initial deployments underscore the state's ambition to foster innovation and secure leadership in emerging technologies, aiming to replicate past industrial successes with new-age companies.

item.title: US Hits Iran's Kharg Island Oil Hub, Spares Infrastructure
item.description: The US struck military targets on Iran's Kharg Island, a critical hub for nearly all its oil exports. The action deliberately avoided oil infrastructure to pressure Tehran while trying to prevent immediate global price shocks. Oil markets are watching closely for further disruption and volatility.

item.title: Avenue Supermarts Names Key Officials for Stock Disclosures from April 1, 2026
item.description: Avenue Supermarts has informed stock exchanges that specific key officials will now handle material information disclosures starting April 1, 2026. This step aims to streamline communication and ensure compliance with SEBI regulations.

item.title: SBI Flags RCOM Unit's Loan as Fraud, Eyes RBI Reporting
item.description: State Bank of India has classified Reliance Telecom Limited's (RTL) ₹125 crore loan as fraud, and will report it to the Reserve Bank of India. This comes as RTL and its parent, Reliance Communications (RCOM), are in corporate insolvency proceedings with resolution plans awaiting NCLT approval, adding a major regulatory challenge.

item.title: India Real Estate: Q1 Leasing Surge Met by AI Risks, Mideast Tensions
item.description: India's commercial property market started 2024 strong with higher leasing than last year. But experts warn that growing AI use and Mideast instability could slow future growth. While Tier-II cities and hotels look promising, office and luxury homes face new challenges, signaling a split market where traditional assets struggle but alternatives gain ground.

item.title: India's Property Market Thrives Amid AI Uncertainty
item.description: India's property market showed strong resilience in early 2026. Office leasing hit historic highs in 2025, driven by global tech centers, with low vacancies and rising rents. The housing market also grew, powered by premium buyers and favorable economic conditions. However, rapid AI adoption creates uncertainty about future office needs and job roles, even as AI promises to boost efficiency.

item.title: MSTR's Bitcoin Holdings Face Debt Pressure and Price Swings
item.description: MicroStrategy has significantly expanded its Bitcoin treasury, now holding over 738,731 BTC. The company recently acquired 17,994 BTC for $1.28 billion, funded by stock issuances. Despite this aggressive accumulation, its cumulative average purchase price of $75,862 per Bitcoin exceeds current market values, and its substantial debt load of over $8.2 billion introduces significant financial leverage and risk. While the stock has shown recent resilience, MSTR's valuation is increasingly tethered to Bitcoin's volatile price action.

item.title: DMart Names Kalpana Unadkat New Chairperson, Bhave Exits
item.description: Avenue Supermarts, which runs the DMart retail chain, has named Kalpana Unadkat as its new Chairperson, starting April 1, 2026. She will replace Chandrashekhar Bhave, who steps down March 31, 2026. Unadkat brings strong expertise in corporate law and governance.

item.title: Thomas Cook India Unlocks 49,196 ESOP Shares for Employees
item.description: Thomas Cook (India) Limited's Nomination and Remuneration Committee has authorized the transfer of 49,196 equity shares under its Employee Stock Option (ESOP) plans. This formalizes the distribution of company stock to employees who have exercised their options, managed through the ESOP Trust. The action clears the way for these shares to be allotted, affecting the company's total outstanding equity.

item.title: NIS Management Subsidiaries' Auditor Resigns; New Firm Search Begins
item.description: NIS Management Limited announced that M/s. Datta Roy & Associates, the statutory auditor for its five subsidiary companies, has resigned effective March 13, 2026. The firm cited professional commitments, stating no other material issues. The company must now appoint new auditors for these subsidiaries.

item.title: Satani Bearings Reclassifies Promoters to Public Shareholders
item.description: Satani Bearings Limited has completed its Open Offer, leading to a significant shift in its ownership. Key promoters are now reclassified as public shareholders as of March 13, 2026, in line with SEBI regulations. This change moves the company towards broader public shareholding.

item.title: Avenue Supermarts Vests 1.5M Stock Options at ₹299; New Shares Possible
item.description: Avenue Supermarts' ESOP Committee has approved the vesting of 15,12,909 stock options for eligible employees. These options, granted in March 2017, can be exercised at ₹299 each until June 13, 2026. While this incentivizes employees, it also presents a potential for share dilution for existing shareholders.

item.title: Fabtech Technologies Pushes Advantek Acquisition Deadline to March 31, 2026
item.description: Fabtech Technologies Cleanrooms Limited has pushed the completion deadline for its acquisition of Advantek Air Systems Private Limited to March 31, 2026. This extension is to finalize necessary documentation and execution formalities. The original acquisition intimation was made on February 12, 2026. The company will continue to update the stock exchange on further developments.

item.title: K&R Rail Engineering CFO Steps Down After Accident Injuries
item.description: K&R Rail Engineering Limited announced Chief Financial Officer Mr. Kulkarni Prahallada Rao will resign on March 5, 2026. His departure stems from injuries sustained in a recent motor vehicle accident. The company, already grappling with financial difficulties and a falling stock price, thanked Mr. Rao for his service.

item.title: India Farmhouse Boom: Wealthy Turn Rural Land into Luxury Assets
item.description: India's affluent are pivoting from urban apartments to managed farmhouse communities, transforming rural land into a formal luxury asset class. This trend is fueled by robust infrastructure development, a surge in ultra-high-net-worth individuals, and evolving lifestyle demands. While offering appreciation potential, the shift also brings risks from formalizing informal land markets.

item.title: JM Financial: Analysts Spot Value Amid Market Turmoil
item.description: Global markets are turbulent due to rising volatility and geopolitical tensions, making investor portfolio management challenging. Vishal Kampani of JM Financial notes these pressures. Despite these risks, JM Financial's current market value appears attractive compared to its peers. Analysts remain cautiously positive on its advisory and wealth management services.

item.title: Shree Cement Kodla Plant Live: 6.5 MTPA Capacity Added
item.description: Shree Cement Limited has fully commissioned its integrated cement plant in Kodla, Karnataka. The expansion adds a 3.50 MTPA cement mill, bringing the facility's total capacity to 6.50 MTPA, strengthening the company's South India presence and pan-India growth plans.

item.title: India's Rs 17,000 Cr Green Steel Push to Cut Imports
item.description: Maharashtra's Chandrapur district will host Rs 17,000 crore in green steel and coal gasification projects. These aim for self-reliance, producing high-value items like ammonia nitrate, green steel, and hydrogen, aligning with India's Atmanirbhar Bharat vision. The initiative seeks to reduce import dependence, foster industrial growth, create over 10,000 local jobs, and potentially spur Rs 3 lakh crore investment in a broader regional steel hub.

item.title: Kerala Ayurveda OKs Share Issue, Debt Swap, Subsidiary Buyout
item.description: Kerala Ayurveda shareholders approved major company changes at their March 14, 2026 meeting. They backed issuing 630,000 shares to Katra Holding, acquiring the full stake in subsidiary Ayurvedagram Heritage Wellness Centre through a share exchange, and updating the 2023 Employee Stock Option Plan.

item.title: Syngene International Tax Penalty Proceedings Dropped, ₹2.78 Crore Relief Stands
item.description: Syngene International Limited announced on March 13, 2026, that the National Faceless Assessment Centre has dropped penalty proceedings initiated for Assessment Year (AY) 2018-19. This follows a prior relief of ₹2.78 crore related to expenses disallowed in earlier assessments. The company anticipates no material financial or operational impact from this resolution.

item.title: Jubilant FoodWorks Fights ₹95 Crore GST Demand, Appeals Decision
item.description: Jubilant FoodWorks Limited has been slapped with a GST demand and penalty order totalling ₹95 crore for FY19-22. The order from CGST & CX, Thane, alleges incorrect service classification. The QSR giant disputes the claim and is appealing, stating no material financial or operational impact is anticipated.

item.title: India Market Volatility: Experts See Opportunity, But Risks Mount
item.description: At the 21st India Business Leader Awards, financiers Utpal Sheth and Deven Choksey advised investors to see market volatility as an opportunity, highlighting business strength and economic resilience. This comes as India VIX levels near 23, the market saw sharp declines on March 13-14, 2026, foreign investors pulled significant funds, and global challenges like geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices persist.

item.title: Karnataka Boosts Green Tech: ₹4.8K Cr for EVs, Biogas Projects
item.description: Karnataka has approved ₹4,824.31 crore for industrial projects expected to create 14,525 jobs. The investments focus on key growth areas like electric vehicle (EV) components and compressed biogas (CBG). This strategic move aims to boost sustainable sectors, diversify the state's economy beyond traditional manufacturing and IT, and strengthen its position against competitors.

item.title: Family Offices Take Active Control in Volatile Markets
item.description: Family offices are changing how they invest. Instead of just being patient investors, they are now taking big stakes in companies. They want to actively run these businesses to boost profits. Their deep knowledge of specific industries and long-term plans help them make money even when markets are shaky. They are acting more like business owners than just investors.

item.title: India Office Leases Hit Record High as Scarcity Beats AI Fears
item.description: India's office market achieved record leasing of 86.4 million sq ft in 2025, proving resilient to AI worries. Brookfield's MD Ashank Kothari noted demand is strong due to a shortage of prime Grade A spaces and India's favorable demographics, projecting 350 million people moving to cities over two decades. This scarcity is boosting rents, even as older buildings become obsolete.

item.title: Tatva Chintan Faces 80% Gas Cut, New Pricing From March 2026
item.description: Tatva Chintan Pharma Chem Ltd has been notified of a government order effective March 2026 that will limit natural gas supply to 80% of past consumption and introduce a new 'Pooled Price' mechanism. This directive overrides existing Gas Sales Agreements. The company is seeking alternative fuels, but the full financial impact remains unquantified.

item.title: NCLT Approves IGESL Demerger, Creating Two Listed Energy Companies
item.description: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has approved the demerger of Inox Green Energy Services Limited (IGESL). The company's power evacuation business will move to a new entity, Inox Renewable Solutions Limited (IRSL), creating two distinct listed companies focused on O&M services and power evacuation respectively.

item.title: Indian IT Faces Slower AI Adoption, Targets Stable Returns
item.description: Fortuna Capital's Sanjay Bhattacharyya believes the current excitement about Artificial Intelligence's immediate impact on India's IT sector might be overblown. He expects AI technologies to spread more slowly, similar to the internet's early days, leading to adjusted growth forecasts. Indian IT companies are now shifting focus through new partnerships and updated business models to navigate this change, aiming for stable, moderate returns and less volatility over the next five years.

item.title: Waaree Energies Launches India's Biggest Solar Ingot-Wafer Plant
item.description: Waaree Energies has initiated its largest integrated ingot and wafer manufacturing facility in Nagpur with a ₹6,200 crore investment. The 10 GW capacity complex aims to bolster India's solar manufacturing ecosystem and reduce import dependence, positioning Waaree against major peers like Adani Solar and Reliance.

item.title: Brigade Enterprises Adds Industrial Real Estate, Boosts Hyderabad Office Space
item.description: Brigade Enterprises is expanding into industrial real estate with Brigade Industrial Park, a 25-acre, 2 million sq ft development in North Bengaluru for aerospace, defense, IT/ITES, and data centers. Its BuzzWorks division also secured a 550-seat lease in Hyderabad for MyComplianceOffice, boosting its Hyderabad footprint to 1.1 lakh sq ft. These steps show a strategy to capture high-growth sector demand and meet demand for flexible offices.

item.title: ABSL AMC Secures 73.1 FY25 ESG Rating from Independent SES
item.description: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Ltd. (ABSL AMC) earned a 73.1 FY25 ESG rating from SEBI-registered SES ESG Research. SES prepared the rating independently using public data, without direct company engagement, underscoring the growing ESG focus for India's asset managers.

item.title: Vraj Iron and Steel Signs 25-Year Solar Power Deal for Cheaper Energy
item.description: Vraj Iron and Steel Limited has finalized a 25-year solar power deal with Chhattisgarh's electricity providers. The agreement for 10.85 MW of renewable energy is set to significantly lower the company's power costs and enhance its commitment to green energy.

item.title: Schaeffler India Names Maximilian Fiedler Director After Shareholder Vote
item.description: Schaeffler India shareholders have approved Maximilian Andreas Fiedler as a Non-Executive Non-Independent Director. The approval, finalized through a postal ballot on March 14, 2026, brings Fiedler's global leadership experience from Schaeffler's Asia/Pacific region to the Indian board, enhancing governance and alignment with the parent group.

item.title: GRM Foodkraft Appoints Independent Director Nidhi to Strengthen Governance
item.description: GRM Foodkraft Private Limited, a subsidiary of GRM Overseas Limited, has named Mrs. Nidhi as a Non-Executive Independent Women Director. Her five-year term, set to begin March 14, 2026, aims to improve corporate governance for the food processing unit. Shareholder approval is required for the appointment.

item.title: GK Energy Appoints Shubham Jain Company Secretary, KMP
item.description: GK Energy Limited has appointed Shubham Suresh Jain as its new Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, effective March 16, 2026. Jain will also hold the designation of Key Management Personnel (KMP). This appointment follows the resignation of the previous Company Secretary and aims to bolster the company's corporate governance and compliance framework. The move is part of GK Energy's ongoing efforts to strengthen its management structure.

item.title: AYE Finance Fined ₹1.49 Lakh by BSE for Late Q3 Results Amid IPO
item.description: AYE Finance Limited has been fined ₹1,48,680 by the BSE for the delayed submission of its unaudited financial results for the quarter ending December 2025. The company attributed the delay to its ongoing Initial Public Offering (IPO) process. AYE Finance is reviewing the notice and will take appropriate steps.
